name: Montage: Beat 11 — The First Three Days

text.content:
Beat 11 — The First Three Days

Act: Act 2 | Type: Montage Scene

Overview

The first three days of the Drowned Road are a punishing descent through flooded cave passages. The party moves through narrow channeled paths, bioluminescent chambers, and carved Root-Rune corridors. Navigation is challenging; the deeper passages are older and stranger. This montage covers exploration, survival, and the first scene with evidence that something is organizing the dead.

Running This Scene

This is a narrative montage — a series of challenges the party faces as a group. Rather than a single roll, play this out as a sequence of scenes where each character contributes.

GM Guidance

  • Go around the table and ask each player how their character is helping.
  • Call for ability checks as appropriate — suggested modified [Resolute←15] (hard).
  • Let creative approaches succeed with good roleplaying, even without a roll.
  • Use the complications below as prompts — not every one needs to come up.
  • The goal is collaborative storytelling, not a pass/fail test.

Relevant Skills:

  • Strong
  • Quick or Discreet
  • Cunning or Cunning
  • Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant
  • Persuasive, Persuasive, or Persuasive

Complications & Challenges

Use these as dramatic beats during the montage. Present them as situations the party must navigate together.

The flooded passages require careful navigation. Water levels shift with underground currents. Kaelen reads Root-Runes on the walls as they descend — the older the Runes, the more information she can extract, but they slow progress.

  • Reason: Map the passage network and mark safe routes vs. flooded dead-ends (the passages branch frequently).
  • Agility: Move through chest-deep flood sections without losing footing or equipment.
  • Intuition: Sense which passages lead deeper into the root-network and which loop back upward.
  • Might: Clear a collapsed section blocking the route, or stabilize a crumbling pillar before it falls on Kaelen.

Escalation

If the scene needs more tension, introduce these additional complications:

The bioluminescent grotto: 33 m across, every inch carved with Root-Runes. Kaelen is overwhelmed — she needs time to process what she's reading. Meanwhile, the party hears movement in the eastern passage: a column of draugr, 30-40 strong, marching in organized formation. The party must stay hidden or be detected.

  • Agility: Find concealment positions in the grotto before the draugr column enters (the passage is narrow — they come through one side).
  • Intuition: Assess the draugr column: not wandering, not hunting. Organized. Marching east. Determine purpose and direction without alerting them.
  • Presence: Keep the group calm and silent while the column passes — 30 draugr, organized formation, 4-minute crossing.
  • Reason: Help Kaelen process what she's discovered quickly enough to share before the draugr are close.

Outcomes

Based on how the party performed, use the following as a guide:

Total Success: The party maps three safe routes and reaches the bioluminescent grotto on schedule. Kaelen has time to read additional Root-Runes and discovers: "These markings are older than the Keeper Order. Someone was here before the Keepers."
Partial Success: Progress is made but the party takes one wrong turn, costing four hours and one Hit Die's worth of endurance from relentless cold and wet.
Failure: The party gets disoriented in flooded chambers. They emerge into the bioluminescent grotto later than planned — exhausted. Each hero loses 1 Hit Die from fatigue.

Total Success: The party conceals successfully. The column passes without detecting them. Kaelen shares: "These are Root-Runes. But this is older. Someone was here before the Keepers." Both revelations land: the dead are organized, and these caves predate Keeper history.
Partial Success: The column passes, but one hero makes noise. Two draugr pause and look back — hold their breath moment. Pass check or one draugr investigates (minor combat: 2 draugr, easily handled but they've been spotted near the column route).
Failure: Draugr detect movement. The column doesn't fully investigate (still organized, not mindless) but a scout breaks off. Combat: 4 Draugr-Thralls + 1 Barrow-Draugr. Column has been alerted to party presence in the caves.
